key_insight: A multiphysics phase-field model links fracture mechanics and piezoresistive sensing in short carbon fiber composites, enabling damage to be inferred from electrical signals.
engineering_relevance: The framework offers a physics-based path for real-time structural health monitoring and inverse crack assessment in self-sensing SCFRP components.
method: Finite-deformation phase-field fracture coupled with viscoelastic-viscoplastic constitutive behavior
Anisotropic crack resistance and conductivity both derived from the second-order fiber orientation tensor
Eight-electrode EIT data fed to a neural network to predict crack length and mechanical compliance
evidence_signal: On held-out configurations, the neural network achieved R2 = 0.99, indicating strong predictive performance across microstructure variations.
watch_item: Performance depends on accurate orientation-tensor fields and calibrated conductivity-damage coupling, which may be challenging to transfer across materials or manufacturing conditions.
paper_title: Data-Driven Structural Health Monitoring of Short Carbon Fiber-Reinforced Polymer Composites via Multiphysics Phase-Field Simulation
